title: [Starmie] Battle Guide
summary: A [Water]/[Psychic] special attacker and spinner with Speed 115 and [natural-cure] — utility plus offensive pressure from one team slot.
strengths: Speed 115 outruns most of the upper metagame
weaknesses: 60/85/85 bulk is fragile against neutral hits
100 SpA is a tier below top-tier breakers

recommendedMoves.reason: Hazard removal plus a Speed boost

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Starmie] is a [Water]/[Psychic] special attacker that combines offense with utility. Speed 115 sits above most of the upper metagame, [natural-cure] provides free status immunity on every switch, and [rapid-spin] gives the team hazard removal without sacrificing an offensive slot. 100 SpA is a tier below dedicated breakers, but the utility package more than compensates.

## Strengths
Speed 115 outruns most unboosted threats, so [Starmie] dictates the Speed tier in most matchups. [natural-cure] is the key utility ability — any status [Starmie] picks up is healed on the next switch, which neutralises [will-o-wisp] and [toxic] strategies. The [hydro-pump] / [psychic] / [ice-beam] combination covers [Grass], [Dragon], [Fighting], and [Ground] threats cleanly, and [rapid-spin] is a one-slot hazard remover plus Speed boost.

## Weaknesses & Answers
60/85/85 bulk is fragile — any neutral priority or 2x coverage threatens a KO. [Dark], [Bug], [Ghost], [Grass], and [Electric] are all 2x weaknesses, and common coverage moves like [knock-off] also strip [leftovers] value. SpA 100 is a tier below dedicated breakers, so [Starmie] struggles to crack through walls without prior chip. Priority users also shut down the Speed advantage.

## Recommended Moveset
[hydro-pump] is the locked [Water] STAB for maximum damage. [psychic] is the secondary STAB that handles [Fighting] and [Poison] threats. [ice-beam] covers [Grass], [Dragon], and [Flying] walls that would resist the primary STABs. [rapid-spin] is the last slot for hazard removal and the Speed boost utility. A [thunderbolt] variant exists for heavier [Water] coverage but drops the [ice-beam] coverage slot.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default for the long-game plan and stacks with [natural-cure] utility. [focus-sash] guarantees one setup turn against priority, and [choice-scarf] reshapes [Starmie] into a pure [revenge] killer. [natural-cure] is the best ability — analytic is only viable for pure offense variants. Tera [Water] maxes [hydro-pump] damage, while Tera [Fairy] flips [Dark], [Bug], and [Dragon] weaknesses defensively.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Starmie] fills the hazard removal and [pivot] role simultaneously, so pair it with setup sweepers that need a clean field. [Fairy] or [Steel] partners cover [Dark] and [Ghost] weaknesses, and a [Ground] partner handles the [Electric] weakness. A priority blocker helps shield [Starmie] setup turns against [revenge] killers.

## Play Patterns
Lead or enter on turn two, [rapid-spin] on a predicted switch to clear hazards, and fire [hydro-pump] or [psychic] on neutral matchups. Use [ice-beam] on [Grass] or [Dragon] pivots, heal status via [natural-cure] by switching out, and Tera on any telegraphed [Dark] or [Bug] move.
